what_it_does: Implements the 'Map' room as an IIFE-registered DNA room with two tabbed SVG views: a radial 'Territory' map of the live design-DNA canon (selectable sectors around an octagon core, with a slide-in detail panel) and a 'One Story' tree diagram of the workshop story (tier cuts, edges, nodes, and an animated 'take a walk' dot/path traversal). It fetches canon, sequence, and workshop.json live, reads motion timing from the tokens face, and wires its own mount/enter/leave lifecycle.
observations: Uses a strict-mode IIFE with ES5 var/functions and registers the room via DNA.rooms.register.
Generates all CSS as a single string returned by css() and injects it in mount().
The Territory view builds an SVG radial map with an octagon core, wedge sectors tinted by DNA.ordinal(i,N,tok), dashed spokes, and labels; clicking a wedge opens a fixed-position panel.
The sector panel lists the sector line, tells, refs chips (faces/docs/code), and child nodes; face chips navigate to the Almanac.
The One Story view lays out a three-row tree, derives tier names from sequence.json, and draws edges and wrapped-title nodes.
The 'take a walk' animation moves a gold dot along a self-drawing SVG path using stroke-dasharray/offset transitions timed by motion_ms.path_draw, with caption crossfades timed by motion_ms.hover.
Wedge click/keydown listeners are attached once to the persistent #mp-terr host via event delegation, so renderTerritory can safely replace innerHTML without stacking handlers.
On enter, it loads data the first time and later rechecks the canon version to redraw if the canon grew.
Includes responsive CSS that turns the panel into a bottom sheet on narrow viewports and makes the story tree horizontally scrollable.
standouts: The registry-timed 'take a walk' animation with deliberate bookend fade-out and one-focal-at-a-time state (button disabled while the dot is active).
The radial canon map generated from live data and tinted by the ordered metal axis tokens.
The explicit, commented avoidance of handler stacking by delegating wedge events from the persistent host.
The use of motion_ms.path_draw and motion_ms.hover as the single source of animation tempo.
conventions: Follows the room lifecycle pattern: mount(el, ctx), enter(), leave(), plus DNA.rooms.register.
Follows live-data fetching with cache-busting for workshop.json and version checking for the canon.
Follows token-driven styling by reading colors and motion values from the registry rather than hardcoding them.
Breaks separation of concerns by embedding HTML, CSS, SVG construction, data fetching, animation orchestration, and navigation in one file.
Uses setTimeout chains and CSS transitions for animation instead of a requestAnimationFrame loop or Web Animations API.
Hardcodes geometry constants and file paths (e.g., 'content/stories/workshop.json').
concerns: The CSS string is built once at mount with HOP embedded, so later changes to motion_ms.path_draw will not update the #mp-dot CSS transition rule even though the inline style in runWalk does use the current HOP.
The walk step timer uses setTimeout(step, HOP + 40), which can drift relative to the CSS transition and may feel slightly out of sync.
If the tokens face is not ready when mount() calls readTempo(), HOP/FADE default to 0 and the CSS rule is locked at 0ms.
The sticky close button in the panel uses float:right, which can overlap long headings or content.
The story-tree layout assumes exactly three depths and may misbehave if the workshop.json shape differs.
No visible error UI for failed fetches beyond a generic 'away' message.
notes: A self-contained room component that turns live canon and story JSON into two interactive SVG visualizations, with careful, token-timed animation choreography.
questions: What is the exact schema of the canon, sequence, and workshop.json data (fields like line, tells, refs, title, role, children)?
What does DNA.ordinal(i, N, tok) return, and what fallback is expected if tok is null?
What are the contracts of C.org.detailStrip, C.org.consequencesBox, C.org.icon, C.esc, C.api.dna, C.tok, and C.navigate?
When is C.tok() guaranteed to be populated relative to mount()?
Is the 'faces' sector the only one intended to contribute a live face-count statistic?
What should happen when the workshop story has fewer than two parents or leaves and the walk cannot be constructed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated purpose: it is room three on paper ground, presenting two drawings of the same law — a radial territory map and a tiered story tree with a guided walk.
apparent_themes: One focal point at a time
Bookend transitions instead of hard cuts
The canon as navigable territory and the story as a traversable path
Paper-ground material palette (ochre, bronze, gold, charcoal)
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to give users both an inspectable structural overview of the design system and a narrative, kinetic reading of one story, with every timing and color choice flowing from the system's own design tokens.
novelty: The 'take a walk' kinetic narrative — a gold dot that descends, traverses, and hops across the story tree — is a distinctive way to turn a static tree diagram into a timed, guided experience.
contribution: It contributes a dual-tab map room pattern, a live-data radial SVG territory renderer with a detail panel, a tiered story-tree layout algorithm, a registry-timed animated walk with bookend fades and one-focal-at-a-time state management, and an event-delegation pattern for dynamic SVG content.
summary_for_embedding: This file implements the 'Map' room as an IIFE-registered DNA room that fetches live canon, sequence, and workshop story data to render an interactive radial SVG territory map with a slide-in sector panel and a tiered story-tree visualization featuring a registry-timed animated 'take a walk' path.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to let users explore the design system's structural canon and one narrative story through two coordinated, motion-timed SVG views.

symbols.does: Scoped DOM query helper.
symbols.description: Returns the first element matching selector s inside rootEl, or null if rootEl is not set.

symbols.does: Scoped DOM query-all helper.
symbols.description: Returns an array of all elements matching selector s inside rootEl, or an empty array if rootEl is not set.

symbols.does: Polar-to-cartesian point formatter.
symbols.description: Computes a point on a circle of radius r at angle a around center (cx, cy) and returns it as a fixed-one-decimal coordinate string.

symbols.does: Builds an SVG annular wedge path.
symbols.description: Constructs a closed SVG path for a sector bounded by inner radius r0, outer radius r1, and angles a0 to a1, using the SVG large-arc flag when needed.

symbols.does: Wraps text into at most two short lines.
symbols.description: Splits a string into words and fills up to two lines of max characters, appending an ellipsis if a third line would be needed.

symbols.does: Returns the room's injected CSS string.
symbols.description: Builds and returns the entire stylesheet for the map room, including layout, territory, panel, story tree, walk animation, and responsive rules.

symbols.does: Renders the radial canon map and side stats.
symbols.description: Builds the SVG territory view from the live canon data, computes node counts, renders a detail strip, and inserts the markup into #mp-terr.

symbols.does: Updates wedge selection visuals.
symbols.description: Toggles the 'on' class on each wedge path based on the current sel index without rebuilding the whole map.

symbols.does: Builds reference chip markup.
symbols.description: Converts a refs object into face, doc, and code chips; face chips are clickable and navigate to the Almanac.

symbols.does: Opens the sector detail panel.
symbols.description: Sets sel to the chosen sector, refreshes wedge highlighting, renders the panel content, and shows the panel and scrim.

symbols.does: Closes the sector detail panel.
symbols.description: Clears the selection, refreshes wedges, and hides the fixed panel and scrim.

symbols.does: Fills the sector panel with details.
symbols.description: Renders the panel header, sector line, tells, reference chips, child nodes, and legend for the selected sector.

symbols.does: Maps tree depth to sequence tier names.
symbols.description: Returns an array of tier labels for each depth, using names from seq.tiers when available or fallback 'cut N' labels.

symbols.does: Builds an SVG story-tree node.
symbols.description: Creates a rounded rectangle with an optional id label and one or two wrapped title lines, positioned at (x, y).

symbols.does: Renders the story tree and walk setup.
symbols.description: Lays out the workshop story tree across three rows, draws cuts, edges, and nodes, and precomputes the 'take a walk' move list if the tree shape allows it.

symbols.does: Crossfades the walk caption.
symbols.description: Fades out the caption, swaps its text, and fades it back in using the FADE duration.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the animated story walk.
symbols.description: Disables the walk button, fades any previous trail, then steps through the precomputed moves while drawing path segments and moving the gold dot.

symbols.does: Starts the dot-and-trail animation sequence.
symbols.description: Positions the dot invisibly at the starting node, reveals it, and kicks off the recursive step function that animates each segment.

symbols.does: Animates one segment of the walk.
symbols.description: Updates the caption, appends a new SVG trail line with a stroke-dashoffset transition, and moves the dot to the next node; schedules the next step after HOP + 40 ms.

symbols.does: Cancels and resets the walk animation.
symbols.description: Clears the walk timer, resets walking state, re-enables the button, removes trail segments, hides the dot, and restores the idle caption.

symbols.does: Switches between Territory and Story tabs.
symbols.description: Toggles tab and view classes, closes the panel when leaving Territory, and stops the walk when leaving Story.

symbols.does: Reads animation timing from design tokens.
symbols.description: Calls C.tok() and assigns HOP from motion_ms.path_draw and FADE from motion_ms.hover, falling back to 0 if tokens are unavailable.

symbols.does: Fetches data and renders both views.
symbols.description: Loads the canon, sequence, and workshop.json in parallel, then calls renderTerritory and renderStory once all promises settle.

symbols.does: Initializes the room DOM and event wiring.
symbols.description: Injects CSS and markup, attaches tab, wedge, scrim, panel, and keyboard listeners, and prepares the persistent container for dynamic renders.

symbols.does: Handles room entry and data refresh.
symbols.description: Triggers the initial load on first entry and, on later visits, reloads or redraws the territory if the canon version has changed.

symbols.does: Cleans up active room state.
symbols.description: Stops any running walk and closes the sector panel when the user leaves the room.
